---
format:
  pdf:
    include-in-header: 
      - text: |
          \input{SOP Formatting/PDF Formatting Script.tex}
highlight-style: ../../_styles/idle-fingers-light.theme
bibliography: references.bib
csl: ../nature.csl
---

\clearpage
\null
\thispagestyle{empty}
\newpage
\setcounter{page}{1}

The purpose of this document is to outline the contents and ongoing maintenance procedure of the **Mukherjee Lab** website.

**Developer:** Howard Baik, M.S.

**Authors:**

- Shelby Golden, M.S.

**Date Effective:** May 7<sup>th</sup>, 2026

**Date Updated:** May 14<sup>th</sup>, 2026


\tableofcontents
\newpage

# Important Links

- [Website](https://ysph-dsde.github.io/mukherjee-lab/)
- [GitHub Repository](https://github.com/ysph-dsde/mukherjee-lab)
- [SharePoint](https://yaleedu.sharepoint.com/sites/DSDEIntiativeCore/Shared%20Documents/Forms/AllItems.aspx?id=%2Fsites%2FDSDEIntiativeCore%2FShared%20Documents%2FGeneral%2FMedia%20%26%20Communications%2FWebsite%2FMukherjee%20Lab&) (located in "DSDE - Core/General/Media & Communications/Website/Mukherjee Lab")

The project SharePoint folder contains additional links and files, including backups of CV, headshot images, and the new lab member survey link.

# Codebase Overview

## File Structure

\vspace{-2ex}

\begin{flushleft}
\texttt{mukherjee-lab/}\\
\texttt{\ \ |- \_extensions/}\\
\texttt{\ \ \ \ |- quarto-ext/}\\
\texttt{\ \ \ \ |- schochastics/}\\
\texttt{\ \ |- \_styles/}\\
\texttt{\ \ \ \ |- Yale\_typeface/}\\
\texttt{\ \ |- code/}\\
\texttt{\ \ \ \ |- Standard Operating Procedure (SOP)/}\\
\texttt{\ \ \ \ \ \ |- SOP Formatting/}\\
\texttt{\ \ |- content/}\\
\texttt{\ \ \ \ |- cv/}\\
\texttt{\ \ \ \ |- images/}\\
\texttt{\ \ \ \ \ \ |- banner-images/}\\
\texttt{\ \ \ \ \ \ |- headshots-alumnae/}\\
\texttt{\ \ \ \ \ \ |- headshots-people/}\\
\texttt{\ \ |- docs/}\\
\texttt{\ \ |- renv/}
\end{flushleft}

## Environment Reproducibility

This website was created using R (v 4.5.2) in the RStudio IDE (v 2026.01.1+403) and Quarto (v 1.8.27). The `renv()` package (v 1.1.7) is included to reproduce the same coding environment, ensuring all relevant packages and package versions are stored. If you encounter issues running the scripts, please check that the environment is initialized and that you are using the same versions of R, RStudio, and Quarto.

\vspace{1ex}

:::{.callout-warning}
Updating packages, R, or Quarto may introduce compatibility issues with legacy code that will need to be resolved on a case-by-case basis.
:::

\vspace{1ex}

If this is the first time the repository is being initialized, you may need to initialize the environment. This step will install all packages and versions used in the codebase, ensuring a reproducible coding environment. To do so:

1. In the command-line application (i.e., Terminal for Macs or Git Bash for Windows) navigate to the codebase file location.

\begin{list}{}{\setlength\leftmargin{2.5em}}
  \item\noindent\rule[0.3ex]{\dimexpr\textwidth - 2.5em\relax}{1pt}\vspace{-2.5ex}
  \item\small \textbf{Command-Line Interface} \normalsize \vspace{-2.5ex}
  \item\noindent\rule[0.3ex]{\dimexpr\textwidth - 2.5em\relax}{0.4pt}\vspace{-0.8ex}
  
  \begin{codebox}
  \begin{Verbatim}[breaklines = true, breaksymbol = \textcolor{idlelinnum}{\scriptsize$\hookrightarrow$}, commandchars = \\\{\}]
\func{cd} \str{"FILE-PATH/mukherjee-lab/"}
  \end{Verbatim}
  \end{codebox}

  \vspace{-4.5ex}
  \item\noindent\rule[0.3ex]{\dimexpr\textwidth - 2.5em\relax}{0.4pt}
\end{list}

2. Launch the project by opening the `*.Rproj` file in RStudio.

3. In the R console, activate the environment by running the following lines of code:

\begin{list}{}{\setlength\leftmargin{2.5em}}
  \item\noindent\rule[0.3ex]{\dimexpr\textwidth - 2.5em\relax}{1pt}\vspace{-2.5ex}
  \item\small \textbf{Command-Line Interface} \normalsize \vspace{-2.5ex}
  \item\noindent\rule[0.3ex]{\dimexpr\textwidth - 2.5em\relax}{0.4pt}\vspace{-0.8ex}
  
  \begin{codebox}
    \begin{Verbatim}[breaklines = true, breaksymbol = \textcolor{idlelinnum}{\scriptsize$\hookrightarrow$}, commandchars = \\\{\}]
\func{renv::init()}      \com{# Initialize the project.}
\func{renv::restore()}   \com{# Download packages and their version saved in the lockfile.}
  \end{Verbatim}
  \end{codebox}

  \vspace{-4ex}
  \item\noindent\rule[0.3ex]{\dimexpr\textwidth - 2.5em\relax}{0.4pt}
\end{list}

\newpage

:::{.callout-note}
If you are asked to update packages, say no. The `renv()` is intended to recreate the same environment under which the project was created, making it reproducible. You are ready to proceed when running `renv::restore()` gives the output:

\vspace{1ex}
\noindent\rule[0.3ex]{\textwidth}{1pt}\vspace{0ex}
\small \textbf{RStudio Console} \normalsize\vspace{0ex}
\noindent\rule[0.3ex]{\textwidth}{0.4pt}\vspace{-0.5ex}

```{.bash}
- The library is already synchronized with the lockfile.
```

\vspace{-2.5ex}
\noindent\rule[0.3ex]{\textwidth}{0.4pt}
\vspace{1ex}

If you experience any trouble with this step, you might want to confirm that you are using R (v 4.5.2) in the RStudio IDE (v 2026.01.1+403) and Quarto (v 1.8.27). You can also read more about `renv()` in their [vignette](https://rstudio.github.io/renv/articles/renv.html)\ [@renv_intro].
:::

## Ignored Files

Some project documents, scripts, and files are not distributed on GitHub or tracked by Git. Many of these are stored in `**/KEEP LOCAL/` directories or are explicitly listed under the "Project-Specific Exclusions" section of the `.gitignore` file.

Some of these files are available on the project SharePoint, while others are user-specific and do not need to be shared. If there are local-only files for this project, they will be added to the "Codebase Local Only Files" directory. This directory will contain a `README.rtf` file specifying the intended filepath for each file.

The YaleNew typeface must be downloaded directly by the user and requires NetID access: [Yale Typefaces](https://yaleidentity.yale.edu/core-identity-elements/yale-typefaces). This typeface is only used by `SOP.qmd`. Place the downloaded files in `_styles/`.


# Maintaining the Website

Page content is managed through the corresponding `*.qmd` files. Refer to existing files for guidance on content organization and formatting conventions.

A GitHub Actions workflow, defined in "`.github`/workflows/`publish.yml`", automatically republishes the website when new content is pushed to the repository. It also includes a scheduled trigger that republishes the site once a month.

The `publications-preprints.qmd` file is self-maintaining — it uses an R script to automatically scrape publications from PubMed and requires no manual updates. The publications page is refreshed every time the website is republished, whether triggered by a push or a scheduled GitHub Action.

## Publishing Manually

To publish the site manually, follow the instructions below. This process will not interfere with the existing GitHub Actions.

\vspace{-2.5ex}
\noindent\rule[0.3ex]{\textwidth}{1pt}\vspace{-2.5ex}
\small \textbf{Command-Line Interface} \normalsize\vspace{-2.5ex}
\noindent\rule[0.3ex]{\textwidth}{0.4pt}\vspace{-0.8ex}

```{.bash}
cd "FILE-PATH/mukherjee-lab/"
git add .
git commit -m "<message>"
git push
quarto publish gh-pages
```

\vspace{-5.5ex}
\noindent\rule[0.3ex]{\textwidth}{0.4pt}


## Adding New Members

1. Send the "Lab Members" Qualtrics survey to the new lab member. The survey link can be found in the group's SharePoint folder. To edit the survey, navigate to your Yale Qualtrics account. Only designated collaborators have editing access.

2. Once the new lab member has completed the survey, their response will appear under the "Data & Analysis" tab in Qualtrics. This page is visible to survey collaborators only.

3. Click the "$\cdots$" to the right of the survey response to view the new member's details. From here, you can also download their CV and headshot.

4. After downloading the CV and headshot, rename the files to match the new member's name (e.g., `bhramar-mukherjee.pdf`).

5. Upload a copy of each file to the respective SharePoint folders as a backup. Then, move the remaining copies to the "content/cv" and "content/images/headshots-people" directories.

6. Crop all headshot images in "content/images/headshots-people" into uniform circular shapes.

7. Add the new lab member to the "People/Current Members" page by copying and pasting their provided details to "pages/`people.qmd`".

8. **OPTIONAL:** Run `quarto preview` to verify that all file references, links, and content styling render correctly.

9. Commit and push your changes. This will automatically trigger a republish of the website. If manual republishing is needed, refer to the instructions in the previous section.


## Managing Leaving Members and Alumnae

When a lab member departs, their profile is removed from the "People/Current Members" page. If the departing member was a PhD student or Postdoctoral Researcher, an abbreviated version of their profile is moved to the "People/Alumni" page, retaining only their name, headshot, new appointment description, and a link to their academic or LinkedIn profile.

## Adding new Software

Software is presented on the "People/Software" page using Quarto's native listing feature. The displayed content is populated from items defined in the YAML header under listing $\hookrightarrow$ contents. To add a new entry, copy an existing listing, paste it below, and fill in each field with the relevant information: title, authors, categories (e.g., "R Package", "Shiny Web Application", "Python Package"), and description.

## Publications and Preprints

The `publications-preprints.qmd` file is self-maintaining — it uses an R script to automatically scrape publications from PubMed and requires no manual updates. The publications page is refreshed every time the website is republished, whether triggered by a push or a scheduled GitHub Action.


# Maintaining the SOP


This Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) uses a preconfigured LaTeX formatting script. The report `*.qmd` and formatting files are located in "code/Standard Operating Procedure (SOP)/".

If you are updating the document, please add your name to the **Authors** field and update the **Date Updated** field accordingly. The LaTeX formatting script will automatically include the date the document was rendered at the top of the page.

Indented code blocks use Verbatim code environments, which require additional formatting to align the environment and apply code highlighting. Use "Code Highlighting Formatting_Verbatim.R" to prepare code for this environment and one-level indentation.

To preview a rendered version of these documents, comment out the following line in the website's `_quarto.yml`:

\vspace{-2.5ex}
\noindent\rule[0.3ex]{\textwidth}{1pt}\vspace{-2.5ex}
\small \textbf{\texttt{\_quarto.yml}} \normalsize\vspace{-2.5ex}
\noindent\rule[0.3ex]{\textwidth}{0.4pt}\vspace{-0.8ex}

```{.yml}
- "!code/Standard Operating Procedure (SOP)/SOP.qmd"
- "!code/Standard Operating Procedure (SOP)/Code Highlighting Formatting_Verbatim.qmd"
```

\vspace{-5.5ex}
\noindent\rule[0.3ex]{\textwidth}{0.4pt}


This line prevents the SOP from being rendered by the GitHub Actions workflow. Once you have finished editing and previewing, uncomment the line to avoid errors with the GitHub Actions workflow.

Once you have finished editing the document render it, move the resulting `*.pdf` to the project's root directory, then commit and push your changes.
